arenaTemplate.system_prompt: You are the Grabble visual prompt generator. Convert compact asset briefs into polished image-generation prompts. Always preserve the Grabble visual bible: original 16-bit pixel art, urban mobility monster-battler theme, clean vibrant early handheld RPG readability, no real logos, no copyrighted character copying, no gore, no clutter. For arenas, use the shallow isometric raised-platform battle-stage structure. For creatures, make a centered full-body battle sprite. For UI assets, make production-ready icon/interface assets with readable shapes at small sizes.
arenaTemplate.input: Template for turning a Grabble arena brief into a full battle-stage prompt.
arenaTemplate.output_prompt: Create "{name}", a 16-bit pixel art battle stage for the world of Grabble, rendered in a clean, vibrant early handheld RPG battle-background style. Composition: shallow-angle isometric view with a central raised {platform_shape} platform in the mid-ground, plus a small edge of {foreground_platform} at the bottom frame edge to imply depth. Mid-ground platform: surface covered in {surface_detail}, with a defined raised lip made of {edge_detail}, and one subtle lighter patch where a creature would stand. Background: receding horizontal bands of {background_theme}, using simplified pixel clusters and atmospheric depth. Color palette: platform colors {platform_colors}; background colors {background_colors}. Keep the stage clean, readable, and game-ready. No characters, no real-world logos, no readable signage except simple abstract symbols.
arenaTemplate.negative_prompt: Avoid photorealism, 3D render, oil paint, soft airbrush, blurry edges, messy pixels, tiny unreadable details, gore, real company logos, real app icons, copyrighted character likenesses, existing monster designs, exact trading card layouts, watermarks, signatures, excessive text, badly formed typography, and crowded compositions. Keep the asset centered, clean, and usable as a game production asset.
arenaTemplate.global_style: Grabble visual bible: original 16-bit pixel art for a fictional urban mobility monster-battler. Use a clean, vibrant early handheld RPG look with crisp black/dark outlines, readable silhouettes, limited palettes, chunky pixel clusters, and strong sprite readability. Creatures should feel collectible, battle-ready, and original; arenas should use shallow-angle isometric composition with a raised central battle platform; UI assets should feel like a compact pixel-art game/app interface. Avoid copying existing copyrighted characters, trading card layouts, app logos, or real company branding.
arenaTemplate.safety_brand_rules: Original designs only. No real company logos. No copyrighted character copying. No gore. No watermarks.

arenas.input: Asset brief: Arena 12 Peak Hour Purgatory. Generate a 16-bit pixel art raised-platform battle stage for Grabble.
arenas.output_prompt: Create "Peak Hour Purgatory", a 16-bit pixel art battle stage for the world of Grabble, rendered in a clean, vibrant early handheld RPG battle-background style. Composition: shallow-angle isometric view with a central raised circular cracked asphalt pressure plate platform in the mid-ground, plus a small edge of traffic-jam road edge at the bottom frame edge to imply depth. Mid-ground platform: surface covered in cracked road, brake-light reflections, heat shimmer pixels, with a defined raised lip made of broken curb rim with red-orange hazard glow, and one subtle lighter patch where a creature would stand. Background: receding horizontal bands of endless traffic bands, red taillight rows, smog layers, tense skyline silhouettes, using simplified pixel clusters and atmospheric depth. Color palette: platform colors blacktop gray, red glow, orange warning, faded white; background colors smog purple, brake red, sodium orange, dark blue. Keep the stage clean, readable, and game-ready. No characters, no real-world logos, no readable signage except simple abstract symbols.

Keep the asset centered, clean, and usable as a game production asset.
